concepts.description: A subfield of artificial intelligence that generates text, images, audio, video, and code using learned patterns from data.
A generative model consisting of a generator and a discriminator trained in a competitive process to create realistic data.
A probabilistic generative model that encodes data into a latent distribution and reconstructs it for smooth sampling.
A neural network architecture based on self-attention that processes tokens in parallel and captures long-range dependencies.
A model trained on massive text corpora to perform language understanding and generation tasks.
A generative AI system that creates images from natural language descriptions.
AI-generated media that replaces a person’s likeness in images, audio, or video using deep neural networks.
An AI system capable of processing and generating multiple data types such as text, image, audio, and video.
A type of machine learning where agents learn to make decisions by receiving rewards or penalties from interactions with the environment.
A training method where the model generates its own labels from unlabeled data to learn representations.
A neural network component that allows models to focus on the most relevant parts of input data.
The process of taking a pre-trained model and adapting it to a specific task using additional training data.
Designing input prompts for AI models to generate the desired output effectively.
A technique for prompting LLMs to reason step-by-step before producing an answer.
The ability of a model to perform tasks it was not explicitly trained on using generalization.
A learning method where a model is trained with only a few examples of a new task.
A type of neural network designed to handle sequential data by maintaining hidden states across time steps.
A specialized RNN that solves the vanishing gradient problem to capture long-term dependencies.
A paper introducing the transformer architecture, revolutionizing NLP and generative AI.
The process of splitting text into smaller units called tokens for language model processing.
A numeric vector representation of text or other data types that captures semantic meaning.
A generative model that iteratively denoises random noise to create realistic images or data.
A technique where human feedback guides model training for safer and more aligned outputs.
A method to compress a large model into a smaller one by transferring knowledge from teacher to student.
An automated method to design optimal neural network architectures for specific tasks.
A training method that pulls similar data points together and pushes dissimilar points apart in representation space.
The task of classifying each pixel in an image into predefined classes.
A series of large language models trained to generate coherent text given input prompts.
A transformer-based model designed for bidirectional language understanding.
A robustly optimized BERT approach that improves pre-training and downstream performance.
Generative AI task that transforms images from one domain to another using learned mappings.
A technique to apply the style of one image to the content of another using neural networks.
Converting written text into spoken audio using generative models.
Converting spoken audio into written text using AI models.
A generative method that synthesizes audio by progressively refining noise signals.
Providing a model with a few example inputs and outputs to guide its behavior on new tasks.
The maximum number of tokens a language model can process in a single input.
When a model learns training data too well, including noise, and performs poorly on new data.
When a model is too simple to capture patterns in training data, resulting in poor performance.
Techniques to artificially increase dataset size by modifying existing samples.
A security risk where malicious inputs manipulate the output of an AI system.
Systematic errors in AI models caused by biased training data or algorithms.
The practice of developing AI systems that are fair, transparent, and aligned with human values.
Techniques to make AI model decisions understandable to humans.
Artificially generated data used for training AI models when real data is scarce or sensitive.
A structured representation of entities and relationships to store and reason about information.
The setting in which an RL agent interacts and learns through rewards and penalties.
Ensuring AI systems' goals and behaviors match human intentions.
AI systems capable of generating software code from natural language or specifications.
AI technique that generates photorealistic 2D or 3D visuals from neural models.
Classifying images into categories without seeing labeled examples, using model generalization.
Mechanism in transformers that relates different positions of a sequence to compute representation.
An entity in RL that learns to make decisions by interacting with the environment.
Using AI models to compress data by generating a compact representation that can reconstruct the original.
A suite of cloud computing services offered by Google running on the same infrastructure used internally by Google.
Provides scalable virtual machines on Google’s infrastructure for compute workloads.
A fully managed platform for building and deploying web applications without managing infrastructure.
Object storage service for storing and retrieving any amount of data with high availability.
Fully managed data warehouse allowing fast SQL queries using Google’s processing power.
Serverless execution environment for building and connecting cloud services without managing servers.
Managed compute platform that automatically scales containerized applications.
Messaging service for sending and receiving messages between independent applications.
Fully managed relational database service supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server.
Fully managed, scalable, globally distributed relational database service.
NoSQL document database built for automatic scaling and high performance.
Managed Kubernetes service to deploy, manage, and scale containerized applications.
Fully managed service for stream and batch data processing using Apache Beam.
Managed Spark and Hadoop service for big data processing.
End-to-end managed machine learning platform for building, training, and deploying ML models.
Suite of AI products to develop and deploy machine learning models.
Pub/Sub is messaging; EventArc allows event-driven architecture across GCP services.
Services for connecting applications and users securely and reliably on Google’s global network.
Distributes incoming traffic across multiple instances for scalability and availability.
Content delivery network to accelerate web and media content for global users.
Securely connects on-premises networks to GCP using IPSec VPN tunnels.
Dedicated physical connection for high-speed private connectivity to GCP.
Identity and Access Management to control who can access which resources on GCP.
Manages users, devices, and apps to secure access to GCP and cloud resources.
Central security and risk management platform for visibility and threat detection.
Manages encryption keys for securing data in Google Cloud services.
Provides visibility into performance, uptime, and health of applications and resources.
Collects and analyzes log data from GCP and applications for monitoring and troubleshooting.
Distributed tracing system to monitor latency and performance of cloud applications.
Debug applications in real-time without stopping or slowing down production workloads.
Infrastructure-as-code tool to automate creation and management of GCP resources.
Tools and reports to manage costs, budgets, and optimize spending on Google Cloud resources.
Agreements defining uptime and reliability commitments from Google Cloud services.
Serverless automatically scales with events; managed services handle infrastructure management.
APIs provided by GCP to access services programmatically.
API management system to create, deploy, and manage APIs on GCP.
Protects applications against DDoS attacks and web attacks.
Data Loss Prevention service to detect and protect sensitive information.
Organizes resources and manages permissions in projects and folders.
Logical containers for organizing GCP resources and access permissions.
Used to organize, filter, and track GCP resources for management and billing.
Automates software deployment using CI/CD best practices on GCP.
CI/CD service to build, test, and deploy applications in GCP.
Securely stores and manages container images and language packages for deployment.
Managed cron service to automate recurring tasks in GCP.
Manages execution, delivery, and scheduling of distributed tasks.
Orchestrates and automates Google Cloud and HTTP-based API services.
Managed workflow orchestration service built on Apache Airflow for scheduling pipelines.
Provides pre-built solutions, APIs, and services to deploy on Google Cloud.
Solutions like Anthos to run and manage applications across multiple clouds and on-premises.
GCP offers compliance certifications and governance tools to meet regulatory requirements.
Official guides, tutorials, and learning paths for using Google Cloud services effectively.
Understanding the fundamentals of cloud computing and its benefits like scalability, cost efficiency, and flexibility.
A comprehensive overview of GCP services and their use cases.
A virtual machine service that provides scalable compute capacity in the cloud.
A fully managed platform for building and deploying applications without managing servers.
Managed Kubernetes service to deploy, manage, and scale containerized applications.
Serverless execution environment for building and connecting cloud services without managing servers.
Fully managed compute platform to run containerized applications on a serverless environment.
Object storage service for storing and retrieving any amount of data with high availability.
High-performance block storage for GCP virtual machines.
Fully managed relational database service supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server.
Serverless, highly scalable data warehouse for analytics and querying large datasets.
Messaging service for sending and receiving messages between independent applications.
Managed workflow orchestration service built on Apache Airflow for scheduling pipelines.
Fully managed, scalable, and strongly consistent relational database service.
Serverless, NoSQL database for building web, mobile, and server applications.
High-performance, scalable NoSQL database for analytical and operational workloads.
Manages users, devices, and apps to secure access to GCP and cloud resources.
Identity and Access Management system to control who has access to GCP resources.
Virtual Private Cloud networks to isolate and connect GCP resources securely.
Distributes incoming traffic across multiple resources to ensure high availability and reliability.
Content delivery network to accelerate web and media content for global users.
Provides visibility into performance, uptime, and health of applications and resources.
Centralized logging to collect, store, and analyze logs from GCP services.
Helps analyze latency and performance issues in distributed applications.
Debug applications in real-time without stopping or slowing down production workloads.
Detects vulnerabilities and security issues in applications hosted on GCP.
Protects applications from DDoS attacks and enforces security policies.
Manages encryption keys to secure data across GCP services.
Provides tools and APIs to train, deploy, and manage machine learning models.
Enables training of custom machine learning models with minimal coding.
Provides pre-trained machine learning models to analyze images.
Automatically translates text between languages using Google's AI models.
Converts text into natural-sounding speech using deep learning models.
Converts spoken language into written text using Google's speech recognition models.
Analyzes and understands text using pre-trained machine learning models.
Provides personalized product or content recommendations for applications.
Managed Spark and Hadoop service for processing large datasets.
Fully managed service for stream and batch data processing.
Cost-optimized messaging service for lightweight and high-throughput workloads.
Fully managed cron job service for scheduling tasks on GCP.
Automates the creation and management of GCP resources using templates.
API management system to create, deploy, and manage APIs on GCP.
Hybrid and multi-cloud platform to run applications across multiple environments.
Helps build, secure, and manage APIs with traffic control, analytics, and monitoring.
Analyzes and visualizes logs to identify patterns and issues in GCP applications.
Provides tools and reports to manage costs, budgets, and optimize spending on Google Cloud resources.
Organizes resources and manages permissions in projects and folders.
Serverless automatically scales with events; managed services handle infrastructure management.
Overview of GCP networking concepts including subnets, routing, and firewall rules.
Techniques for integrating on-premises infrastructure with cloud resources.
Understanding service level agreements and compliance standards for Google Cloud.
The hierarchy of Google Analytics consisting of Account, Property, and View to organize data collection.
A website, app, or device whose data is collected and tracked in Google Analytics.
A filtered perspective of data for a property, allowing you to focus on specific traffic or metrics.
JavaScript code snippet installed on a website to collect user interaction data.
A group of user interactions on a website within a given timeframe, typically 30 minutes by default.
A unique visitor to the website, identified by cookies and tracked across sessions.
Recorded each time a user loads a page on a website being tracked by Google Analytics.
A way to measure user interactions like clicks, downloads, and video plays beyond pageviews.
A completed action defined as important for business, such as a purchase, signup, or download.
The percentage of users who complete a goal compared to total users or sessions.
The percentage of single-page sessions where no further interaction occurs.
Data showing how users arrive at a website, including channels like Organic, Paid, Referral, or Direct.
The origin from which a visitor reaches your website, such as search engines, social media, or campaigns.
The type of traffic, such as organic, CPC, referral, email, or none (direct).
Using UTM parameters to identify specific marketing campaigns and their effectiveness.
A user-defined attribute to segment and analyze data beyond default metrics.
A user-defined numeric measurement to track specific interactions on a website.
Splitting data into subsets to analyze specific groups of users or behavior patterns.
Monitor active user activity on your website as it happens, including location and page views.
Data about your website visitors, including demographics, interests, devices, and behavior.
Insights into user interactions on the website, including pages visited, content, and events.
Analysis of traffic sources to understand where users are coming from.
Insights into goal completions, ecommerce tracking, and overall business objectives.
Tracking of product sales, revenue, and transaction data for online stores.
Advanced tracking for product impressions, shopping behavior, and checkout steps.
Metrics related to page load time and user experience on your website.
Data about how users use your website's internal search feature and what they look for.
Shows how different channels contribute to conversions over multiple touchpoints.
Rules defining how credit for conversions is assigned to different channels.
The first page a user visits in a session, often a key entry point for campaigns.
The last page a user visits before leaving the website during a session.
Visual representation of the paths users take through your website.
Grouping users based on shared characteristics or acquisition date to track behavior over time.
Notes added to reports to mark specific events, changes, or campaigns.
Tags added to URLs to track the performance of campaigns in Google Analytics.
Visitors who arrive at your website from other domains or websites.
Visitors coming from unpaid search results on search engines.
Traffic generated from paid advertising campaigns on search engines.
Visitors who enter your website URL directly or from bookmarks.
Visitors coming from social media platforms like Facebook, LinkedIn, or Twitter.
Measuring user interactions and traffic from email marketing campaigns.
Classifying visitors based on device type: desktop, tablet, or mobile.
Information about the browser and operating system used by visitors.
Tracking user locations by country, region, or city.
Information about age, gender, and other user characteristics.
User interests based on browsing and engagement data.
User-defined reports to focus on specific metrics and dimensions.
Collection of widgets and reports for at-a-glance analysis.
Automatic alerts about significant changes in your website data.
Ability to upload external data to combine with Google Analytics metrics.
Using a subset of data to estimate metrics when working with large datasets.
Rules applied to views to include or exclude traffic data.
Custom subsets of users or sessions to analyze behavior.
Notes you can add to reports to explain trends or events.
